jokes.text: So leave a message, unless this is Carlton, in which case, don't.
jokes.setup: Standard voicemail greeting setup
jokes.punchline: Specific exclusion of Carlton from leaving messages
jokes.explanation: Classic Will/Carlton dynamic established efficiently in voicemail format. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Will, it's Carlton. I got an emergency.
jokes.setup: Will just said not to leave messages if you're Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton immediately does exactly what Will said not to do
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate payoff to the previous joke. Carlton's obliviousness is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Who was that? - It's just a wrong number, baby.
jokes.setup: We just heard Carlton's emergency message
jokes.punchline: Will dismisses his cousin as a 'wrong number' to his date
jokes.explanation: Will's priorities clearly established - romantic evening over family obligations.

jokes.text: Now, where was I? Oh, there I was.
jokes.setup: Trying to resume romantic mood after interruption
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on returning to seduction mode
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that keeps the scene moving but isn't particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Jazzy, maybe we should revise our plans. You're right, my pet. Let's unpack after we eat.
jokes.setup: Jazz interrupting romantic dinner
jokes.punchline: Jewel's casual acceptance reveals they're moving in
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that escalates Will's predicament from guests to permanent residents.

jokes.text: I told the landlord, I don't you repainting our apartment. Those stains have memories.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they can't stay at their own apartment
jokes.punchline: Sentimental attachment to apartment stains
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - finding meaning in gross domestic details. Strong craft and quotable.

jokes.text: Jazz, homey, look, now, you know, mi casa es su casa. But, look, tonight, mi casa es mi casa.
jokes.setup: Using Spanish hospitality phrase
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting it with the same phrase structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy. The structure of using the same phrase to mean opposite things is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, I do. I'm really hungry.
jokes.setup: Will trying to convince his date not to leave
jokes.punchline: She's leaving because she's hungry, not because of the situation
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that deflates romantic tension with practical concern.

jokes.text: Hey, baby, baby, I can have some Rotiserie Gold here in six minutes.
jokes.setup: Trying to keep date from leaving for food
jokes.punchline: Specific fast food reference as romantic save
jokes.explanation: Brand name specificity adds realism but the joke is fairly standard desperate romantic gesture.

jokes.text: Munchkin, perhaps our visit is a little ill-timed.
jokes.setup: Just ruined Will's romantic evening completely
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor. The formal language contrasts nicely with the chaos they've caused.

jokes.text: Don't worry, me and Jewel could sleep out on a park bench. I hear rain is nature's moisturizer.
jokes.setup: Offering alternative to staying at Will's
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous positive spin on being homeless
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jazz character moment - his ability to find silver linings in terrible situations. 'Nature's moisturizer' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Either his head's getting smaller or his butt's getting bigger.
jokes.setup: Watching Carlton storm off
jokes.punchline: Visual assessment of Carlton's proportions
jokes.explanation: Classic Will roast of Carlton. Good visual comedy inference and economical delivery.

jokes.text: There's a whole army of these action figures around my bed. They're fertility gods, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton confused about figurines around the bed
jokes.punchline: They're fertility gods, revealing the purpose
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with Carlton as innocent straight man. The fertility gods detail adds absurd specificity.

jokes.text: Wait. Y'all trying to have a baby?
jokes.setup: Putting together the fertility gods clue
jokes.punchline: Will's dawning realization
jokes.explanation: Standard realization beat that moves the plot forward. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: ...but it's kind of close quarters here... so you think y'all can put a boom-shaka-lock on it for a minute?
jokes.setup: Asking them to stop having sex in his apartment
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism 'boom-shaka-lock'
jokes.explanation: Excellent original euphemism that's both funny and character-appropriate. Very quotable and creative.

jokes.text: Not on my Posturepedic, bud.
jokes.setup: Discussing the baby-making mission
jokes.punchline: Specific brand name protection of his mattress
jokes.explanation: Brand specificity adds realism and humor. Good escalation of Will's boundary-setting.

jokes.text: The doctor at the clinic says this is when I'm at my most fertile... which reminds me, sweetie. I took my temperature, all systems go.
jokes.setup: Explaining the timing urgency
jokes.punchline: TMI about fertility tracking
jokes.explanation: Standard TMI comedy. The 'all systems go' adds some personality but it's fairly predictable cringe humor.

jokes.text: Permission to board the mother ship.
jokes.setup: Continuing the fertility discussion
jokes.punchline: Space mission metaphor for sex
jokes.explanation: Creative and perfectly Jazz-like metaphor. The space mission angle is both absurd and somehow fitting for his character.

jokes.text: Carlton, relax, I'll get you some new Barney sheets.
jokes.setup: Carlton upset about his bed being used
jokes.punchline: Revealing Carlton has Barney bedsheets
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that infantilizes Carlton. Barney sheets are exactly what Carlton would have.

jokes.text: Hey, Jazz. This ain't funny, man. I command you to stop.
jokes.setup: Jazz and Jewel continuing despite protests
jokes.punchline: Will's futile authoritative command
jokes.explanation: The desperation escalates nicely but 'I command you' is a fairly standard desperation beat.

jokes.text: Roll away from the woman.
jokes.setup: Previous command failed
jokes.punchline: More specific military-style order
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with military precision language. 'Roll away from the woman' has a nice absurd specificity.

jokes.text: I can't take this anymore. I'm gonna go sleep with Mom and Dad.
jokes.setup: Carlton driven to desperation by the noise
jokes.punchline: Childish solution of sleeping with parents
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - reverting to childhood solutions. The phrasing is perfectly innocent/infantile.

jokes.text: Careful Uncle Phil doesn't roll over on you again.
jokes.setup: Carlton going to sleep with parents
jokes.punchline: Reference to Uncle Phil's weight and previous incidents
jokes.explanation: Classic Uncle Phil weight joke with the implication this has happened before. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: But since you ain't getting us no wedding present, this is the least you could do.
jokes.setup: Acknowledging they're an inconvenience
jokes.punchline: Justifying it as replacement for wedding gift
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jazz logic - finding a way to make his intrusion Will's fault. Perfect character reasoning.

jokes.text: What are you talking about, man? I gave you a wedding. Take that to the bank.
jokes.setup: Jazz claiming no wedding present
jokes.punchline: Will claiming the venue was the gift
jokes.explanation: Good counter-argument from Will. 'Take that to the bank' adds attitude and references their previous wedding episode.

jokes.text: I think you mean 'having a baby,' Jazz. Trust me on this.
jokes.setup: Jazz talking about 'making a baby being blessed'
jokes.punchline: Will correcting that having the baby is the blessed part
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay that distinguishes the act from the outcome. Will's world-weariness shows through.

jokes.text: Yo, crank it up!
jokes.setup: Jazz talking about her being his delicate flower
jokes.punchline: Jewel's aggressive sexual command
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Jazz's romantic description and Jewel's direct approach. Excellent timing and character subversion.

jokes.text: Hey, take it easy in there, we're not insured.
jokes.setup: Loud sounds from their activities
jokes.punchline: Practical concern about property damage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke that imagines the physical intensity. Insurance concern adds practical humor.

jokes.text: Well, I don't think it's a very good idea for Jazz and Jewel to reproduce. Right, big guy. The prisons are crowded enough.
jokes.setup: Carlton expressing concern about their reproduction
jokes.punchline: Will's dark joke about their offspring's future
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Jazz's influence on children. Good character dynamic with Carlton setting up the darker punchline.

jokes.text: Let's see, there's 'some kind of perfume or something,' from Miss Hilary. 'A book might be neat,' from Miss Ashley. And, 'here's 20 bucks and I expect some change back' from Master Carlton.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey listing their gift 'specifications'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly lazy and cheap gift descriptions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating specificity that perfectly captures each character. Carlton expecting change back is the perfect topper.

jokes.text: 'A book might be neat,' from Miss Ashley.
jokes.setup: Following up Hilary's lazy gift suggestion
jokes.punchline: Ashley's slightly better but still vague suggestion
jokes.explanation: Part of the escalating lazy gift sequence. Ashley's is marginally better than Hilary's but still thoughtless.

jokes.text: And, 'here's 20 bucks and I expect some change back'... ...from Master Carlton.
jokes.setup: Completing the trilogy of lazy gift suggestions
jokes.punchline: Carlton's cheapness even when someone else is shopping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - cheap and controlling even when delegating. The expectation of change back is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I got my Mom something she gonna love. She always wanted to get my baby shoes bronzed, but she never got around to it. So I hooked her up.
jokes.setup: Will claiming he did his own thoughtful shopping
jokes.punchline: Bronzing his own adult shoes instead of baby shoes
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect Will logic. The sentiment is sweet but the execution is hilariously wrong.

jokes.text: Those are your baby shoes? Well, yeah. I had big feet.
jokes.setup: Carlton questioning if those are baby shoes
jokes.punchline: Will's matter-of-fact explanation about having big feet as a baby
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that maintains Will's logic while being absurd. His casual delivery sells it.

jokes.text: You know what they say about a guy with big feet. No, what? They be saying, 'Damn, you got some big feet.'
jokes.setup: Classic setup for sexual innuendo about big feet
jokes.punchline: Completely literal interpretation instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that subverts the expected sexual joke with pure literalism. Perfect Will Smith delivery and timing.

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right. Feel ashamed of your sorry selves. You feel ashamed of your sorry selves.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil shaming the kids
jokes.punchline: Will piling on with repetitive shame commands
jokes.explanation: Will jumping on the moral high ground bandwagon. The repetition adds some humor but it's fairly basic.

jokes.text: Makes me wanna do something special for Mrs. Banks myself. So you don't want me to get the usual dozen roses? No, that's good enough.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey inspired to do something special
jokes.punchline: Phil's hypocrisy about the roses being good enough
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy reveal. Phil preaching thoughtfulness while being lazy himself is classic sitcom irony.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm surprised y'all can walk.
jokes.setup: Jazz and Jewel returning from their activities
jokes.punchline: Comment on their physical state
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual humor callback. Functional but not particularly creative or memorable.

jokes.text: Your levity is inappropriate. We've been at the free clinic all morning... to find out why my oven is as yet bunless.
jokes.setup: Jazz being serious about clinic visit
jokes.punchline: Formal language mixed with baking metaphor for pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - overly formal language with creative metaphor. 'As yet bunless' is particularly good.

jokes.text: Y'all haven't been baking long. Hey, and I'm sure, if you stick with that recipe... you'll get the ingredients mixed up...
jokes.setup: Continuing the baking metaphor
jokes.punchline: Extended baking advice that becomes sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Attempts to continue Jazz's metaphor but gets a bit convoluted. The innuendo is there but not as clean as it could be.

jokes.text: I mean, we ain't never gonna have a baby... because my little soldiers ain't marching.
jokes.setup: Jazz revealing fertility problems
jokes.punchline: Military metaphor for sperm
jokes.explanation: Classic euphemism that fits Jazz's character. The military metaphor is well-established comedy territory but executed well.

jokes.text: Jazz, didn't I tell you to not be wearing them bikini drawers? You told me not to wear them in the streets. You ain't tell me not to wear them.
jokes.setup: Jazz's fertility problems
jokes.punchline: Revealing underwear advice and Jazz's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Jazz's literal thinking. The bikini underwear detail adds specificity and explains the fertility issue.

jokes.text: We can't afford another free clinic.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting second opinion at free clinic
jokes.punchline: Can't afford free services
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Jazz's financial situation. The contradiction is mathematically impossible but emotionally true.

jokes.text: Never heard two people try harder.
jokes.setup: Will expressing sympathy for their efforts
jokes.punchline: Reference to their loud activities
jokes.explanation: Callback to their noisy activities. Functional humor that acknowledges the running gag.

jokes.text: I want your little soldiers.
jokes.setup: Jewel asking Will for help
jokes.punchline: Direct request for sperm using Jazz's military metaphor
jokes.explanation: Direct and uncomfortable request that uses established metaphor. The bluntness creates good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: What do you say? Can I have your baby? I'd really appreciate it.
jokes.setup: Previous request for soldiers
jokes.punchline: Casual, polite request for baby donation
jokes.explanation: The casual politeness of asking for a baby makes it funnier. 'I'd really appreciate it' is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: I don't want your body, Will. I want your little soldiers.
jokes.setup: Will misunderstanding her request
jokes.punchline: Clarifying she wants sperm, not sex
jokes.explanation: Good clarification that maintains the metaphor while making the request more clinical and awkward.

jokes.text: Do I look like the U.N?
jokes.setup: Jewel asking for his 'little soldiers'
jokes.punchline: United Nations peacekeeping force reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of the military metaphor to international peacekeeping. Creative and economical response.

jokes.text: You hear that, munchkin? He's giving us a VCR too. He's so full of love.
jokes.setup: Will offering VCR as alternative to baby request
jokes.punchline: Jazz interpreting it as additional gift, not replacement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz logic that misinterprets Will's alternative offer as an addition. 'Full of love' ties it together nicely.

jokes.text: Remember that time we went to go play ball... and your shoelace broke and I gave you mine? Same thing.
jokes.setup: Jazz explaining why Will should help
jokes.punchline: Equating shoelace lending with sperm donation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Jazz's reasoning. The complete mismatch of scale is hilarious and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I mean, you got a good heart. And you got a lot of smarts... and you're tall, and you got good teeth. Of course, we'll be praying he gets my ears.
jokes.setup: Jazz listing Will's good qualities for fatherhood
jokes.punchline: Hoping the baby gets Jazz's ears instead
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that starts with compliments then reveals Jazz's one vanity. The prayer reference adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: How about a toaster?
jokes.setup: Jazz and Jewel's emotional appeal for a baby
jokes.punchline: Will's increasingly desperate alternative offers
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Will offering appliances instead of babies. Simple but effective escalation.

jokes.text: Yes, so think about sleeping on the couch for a while.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil giving roses and saying it's the thought that counts
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv's threat about the couch
jokes.explanation: Classic married couple dynamic. The parallel structure with Phil's 'thought that counts' is well-constructed.

jokes.text: Hilary, you can't even make change. Oh, yeah? Well, you can't even make... Oh, shut up.
jokes.setup: Hilary claiming she made something
jokes.punchline: Ashley's putdown and Hilary's failed comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's incompetence and Ashley's wit. Hilary's failed comeback is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Don't try to talk. Let's all just share this moment.
jokes.setup: Family's reaction to Hilary's presentation
jokes.punchline: Hilary misinterpreting their speechless horror as appreciation
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary obliviousness. The gap between her perception and reality is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: You're a little embarrassed, aren't you? Don't worry, we're all professionals here.
jokes.setup: Will at sperm bank looking uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Worker's professional reassurance
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward situation humor. The professional's matter-of-fact approach contrasts with Will's discomfort.

jokes.text: You kidding? I come here once a week. Why? Well, a lot of couples wanna have babies and can't. I like to think of myself as nature's middleman.
jokes.setup: Explaining his frequent visits
jokes.punchline: 'Nature's middleman' description
jokes.explanation: Creative and memorable phrase that captures the character's self-importance about his donations.

jokes.text: Kind of like a Johnny Appleseed without the apples, huh?
jokes.setup: Regular customer calling himself nature's middleman
jokes.punchline: Johnny Appleseed comparison with obvious substitution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that's both clever and inappropriate. Perfect economy and the historical reference adds wit.

jokes.text: 'Heard you had a stroke'? It was the closest we could find. You know, for some reason, Hallmark has overlooked this occasion.
jokes.setup: Jazz giving Will an inappropriate greeting card
jokes.punchline: Hallmark not making sperm donation cards
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor about greeting card gaps. The brand name reference and absurd situation work perfectly together.

jokes.text: No one must know of my secret shame. The oath.
jokes.setup: Jazz asking Will to keep the arrangement secret
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a dramatic secret pact
jokes.explanation: Jazz's tendency toward dramatic overstatement works well here. The formality contrasts with the situation.

jokes.text: You know, that's not exactly the image I need right now.
jokes.setup: Jazz saying he could kiss Will
jokes.punchline: Will's concern about appropriate mental imagery
jokes.explanation: Good practical concern that acknowledges the awkward timing. Will's awareness of the situation adds realism.

jokes.text: That boy down there has got good genes. That boy got good genes. You can't play no ball like that without having no good genes.
jokes.setup: Will's fantasy continues
jokes.punchline: His mother's repetitive pride about genetics
jokes.explanation: Good character voice for Will's mother, though the repetition is a bit excessive. The pride is believable and touching.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Will, but you don't belong here.
jokes.setup: Will trying to join the celebration
jokes.punchline: Being rejected by his own biological son
jokes.explanation: Excellent emotional escalation that gets to Will's core fear. The formal rejection is both polite and devastating.

jokes.text: Ready, Mr. Smith? No, I'm not. I don't belong here.
jokes.setup: Clinic worker ready to proceed
jokes.punchline: Will using his fantasy son's words to back out
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows the fantasy affected Will's decision. The echo of dialogue ties the sequence together well.

jokes.text: Oh, we didn't order. This was here when we sat down. They're cheap tippers too. Less than 15 percent.
jokes.setup: Will commenting on restaurant service
jokes.punchline: Judging previous customers' tipping based on abandoned meal
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about restaurant detective work. The specific percentage adds believable detail.

jokes.text: Babies. I mean, they all bald and stanked up and nasty...
jokes.setup: Will trying to talk them out of having kids
jokes.punchline: His unflattering description of babies
jokes.explanation: Standard anti-baby humor that fits Will's current mindset. The descriptive language is vivid but not particularly original.

jokes.text: How the hell did that happen?
jokes.setup: Learning Jewel is pregnant naturally
jokes.punchline: Will's confusion about the conception
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction that moves the plot but isn't particularly clever. Functional comedy beat.

jokes.text: Hey, listen, they having a kid and I ain't had nothing to do with it.
jokes.setup: Will celebrating their pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing his non-involvement
jokes.explanation: Will's relief is palpable and funny. The emphasis on non-involvement after the whole episode is satisfying.

jokes.text: I know we invited you to dinner, but would you mind paying for it? I saw my picture over the cash register.
jokes.setup: Jazz inviting Will to celebratory dinner
jokes.punchline: Asking him to pay and revealing he's banned from restaurant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - inviting someone to dinner he can't pay for at a place that banned him. Classic escalating imposition.

jokes.text: Come on, Jazzy, let's hit the salad bar. I brought my big purse.
jokes.setup: Going to celebrate at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Jewel planning to steal from salad bar
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Jewel's practical approach to free food. The 'big purse' detail is perfect.

jokes.text: You know what they say. 'Easy come, easy go.'
jokes.setup: Jewel saying Will might be disappointed
jokes.punchline: Using 'easy come' phrase in sperm donation context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre that works on multiple levels. Perfect economy and the timing caps off the episode theme perfectly.

jokes.text: No, you... No, thanks, man. You know what? You know what? I just lost my damn appetite.
jokes.setup: Waiter offering to hear specials
jokes.punchline: Will's loss of appetite after the day's events
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat that shows Will is still processing the day. The repetitive 'you know what' shows his scattered state.
